what_it_does: Defines a browser-global OpenAI image service on window.cvOpenAI, with persisted settings, cached image-model discovery, model capability metadata, image generation/edit/variation calls, Responses API image calls, streaming image handling, prompt brand enrichment, connection testing, and simple chat intent classification.
observations: The file is an IIFE that keeps settings, listener sets, model cache helpers, and request helpers private while exposing a single window.cvOpenAI object.
Settings and model discovery cache are stored in localStorage under cvstudio:cvOpenAI keys.
A static model registry describes known image model families, sizes, qualities, formats, background support, moderation options, prompt limits, and endpoint capabilities.
Model discovery combines /v1/models results with pattern matching, static registry entries, inferred capabilities, and a 24-hour cache.
Image generation and edit calls validate configuration, prompt presence, model support, size, quality, output format, background, moderation, and streaming options before calling OpenAI endpoints.
Streaming image responses are parsed as SSE-style data blocks and can report partial images through opts.onPartial.
Prompts are automatically appended with ConceptV brand style language unless brandEnrich is false or the prompt already mentions conceptv.
The exported service includes both classic Images API helpers and a Responses API image_generation tool helper.
Errors are normalized into Error objects with cv-openai/* codes in many paths.
standouts: The static capability registry is used as both offline UI data and runtime validation metadata.
Unknown image model ids can still appear and receive inferred capability defaults from family-name patterns.
The service centralizes browser-side image generation, editing, variation, model listing, settings, and Vi intent classification behind one global surface.
conventions: Uses closure-scoped constants and functions, then exports a compact service object on window.cvOpenAI.
Uses localStorage for durable browser state and Set-based listener subscriptions for settings and model-cache changes.
Uses capability objects to decide endpoint parameters instead of branching only on model ids at call sites.
Uses cv-openai/* error codes for caller-facing failure classification.
Uses optional chaining and defensive try/catch blocks around browser storage, listeners, response parsing, and SSE event parsing.
Falls back to static or inferred data when API discovery is unavailable or incomplete.
concerns: The API key is stored in localStorage and sent from browser fetch calls unless a proxy URL is configured.
Several catch blocks intentionally swallow errors, which can hide storage, listener, JSON, and SSE parsing failures from callers.
generateImage enforces maxPromptChars, while editImage builds the prompt without the same prompt-length check.
validateSize returns a reason, but generateImage and editImage silently substitute a fallback size instead of surfacing the validation reason.
streamSSE reads only the first data: line in each SSE block and ignores malformed event payloads.
responsesImage uses a hard-coded default responses model string separate from the image model registry.
classifyIntent is regex-based and may classify based on broad verb and noun matches without context.
notes: This file is the browser-facing OpenAI image service: settings, model capabilities, image requests, streaming, and exported helpers live together here.
questions: Is localStorage the intended place for the API key in this browser context?
Should invalid image sizes be reported to callers instead of silently replaced with a fallback?
Should editImage enforce the same maxPromptChars limit as generateImage?
Is the hard-coded responsesModel default meant to be configurable through settings?
Should streamSSE preserve or report parse errors from malformed event blocks?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it acts as a shared OpenAI service for image calls, model preferences, settings, errors, and Vi image intent support, with added capability discovery and prompt enrichment behavior.
intention_signals: The file appears intended to give the app one consistent image-AI integration point that can survive model churn, provide UI-ready capability metadata, and keep generated imagery aligned with ConceptV styling.
novelty: It combines static model capability metadata, live model discovery, inferred support for unknown model ids, brand prompt enrichment, streaming partial-image handling, and multiple OpenAI image workflows in one browser-global service.
contribution: This file contributes a reusable client-side OpenAI image service layer with persisted configuration, model capability interpretation, request normalization, image endpoint wrappers, streaming handling, and prompt-branding logic that a fused system would keep as an integration and capability-mapping substrate.
summary_for_embedding: A browser-global OpenAI image service that persists settings, manages image model capabilities and cache, validates request options, calls generation/edit/variation/Responses image endpoints, streams partial images, enriches prompts with ConceptV style, and exposes helpers via window.cvOpenAI.
intention_for_embedding: This was for centralizing ConceptV image-AI configuration and calls so app canvases and Vi chat use the same model metadata, settings, error handling, and brand-aligned prompt behavior.

symbols.does: Checks whether a model id should be treated as image-capable.
symbols.description: This function accepts ids explicitly present in MODEL_REGISTRY and otherwise tests them against known image-model naming patterns. It is used to filter /v1/models results into the service's image model list.

symbols.does: Builds a capability object for an unregistered model id.
symbols.description: This function chooses a known family template for gpt-image and dall-e ids, then replaces display metadata with the unknown id. For other image-like ids, it returns conservative generation-only defaults.

symbols.does: Returns registered or inferred capability metadata for a model id.
symbols.description: This is the lookup boundary for model capability decisions. It prefers exact registry entries and delegates unknown ids to inferCapabilities so downstream code always receives a capability object.

symbols.does: Loads persisted OpenAI service settings from localStorage.
symbols.description: This function reads the settings key, parses JSON, and overlays stored values onto DEFAULT_SETTINGS. On missing or invalid storage data it returns a fresh default-settings object.

symbols.does: Persists settings and notifies settings subscribers.
symbols.description: This function writes the supplied settings object to localStorage when possible, then calls each listener with the new settings. Storage and listener errors are caught and ignored.

symbols.does: Loads the cached model-discovery payload from localStorage.
symbols.description: This helper parses the model cache and requires a numeric fetchedAt field before accepting it. Invalid, missing, or unparsable cache data produces null.

symbols.does: Persists model-discovery cache data and notifies model subscribers.
symbols.description: This function writes a model payload to localStorage and then calls registered model listeners with that payload. It mirrors saveSettings with defensive catch blocks around storage and listener calls.

symbols.does: Resolves an API path to either the configured proxy URL or the OpenAI API origin.
symbols.description: This function trims a trailing slash from settings.proxyUrl and appends the request path when a proxy is configured. Without a proxy it builds a URL under https://api.openai.com.

symbols.does: Builds authorization headers for OpenAI requests.
symbols.description: This helper creates a Bearer token header from settings.apiKey, merges any extra headers, and adds OpenAI-Organization when configured. It is used by direct JSON, FormData, model-list, and connection-test requests.

symbols.does: Reports whether an API key is present and starts with sk-.
symbols.description: This function is a lightweight configuration gate for network operations. It checks only the current in-memory settings object and the key prefix.

symbols.does: Creates an Error with a service-specific code.
symbols.description: This helper wraps a message and optional code into an Error object. When no code is supplied it uses cv-openai/unknown.

symbols.does: Extracts a readable error message from a failed fetch response.
symbols.description: This async helper reads response text, tries to parse it as JSON, and returns nested error.message, message, the raw body, or an HTTP status fallback. It is used before throwing cv-openai/http-* errors.

symbols.does: Returns cached or freshly fetched image-capable model metadata.
symbols.description: This function checks the local model cache and its 24-hour freshness window, optionally bypassed by opts.force. Without a configured key it saves and returns static registry data; with a key it fetches /v1/models, filters likely image models, sorts by tier metadata and name, appends missing registry ids, attaches capabilities, caches the payload, and returns it.

symbols.does: Validates an image size string against model capability rules.
symbols.description: This function accepts auto immediately, parses WIDTHxHEIGHT strings, and applies custom-size constraints when the model supports custom sizes. For non-custom models it checks membership in the model's declared sizes and returns an ok/reason result.

symbols.does: Applies ConceptV brand prompt enrichment unless disabled or already branded.
symbols.description: This function appends a fixed style suffix describing warm paper, gold ink, ivory canvas, warm ink, bronze accents, natural light, and warm tones. It skips enrichment when opts.brandEnrich is false or the prompt already contains conceptv.

symbols.does: Maps a requested quality value to a model-supported value.
symbols.description: This helper returns undefined when the model declares no qualities, preserves supported requested values, translates generic high/medium/low terms to DALL-E-style values when available, and otherwise returns the first declared quality.

symbols.does: Creates images through the /v1/images/generations endpoint.
symbols.description: This async function validates configuration, prompt text, prompt length, model capabilities, size, quality, output format, compression, background, moderation, user, count, and optional streaming. It either delegates to the streaming helper or posts JSON to the generations endpoint and normalizes returned images.

symbols.does: Edits or composes images through the /v1/images/edits endpoint.
symbols.description: This async function validates key presence, source image presence, prompt presence, edit support, reference image count, size, and optional generation parameters. It builds FormData for one or more images plus an optional mask, supports streaming where available, and normalizes returned images.

symbols.does: Starts a streaming JSON image request.
symbols.description: This helper wraps streamSSE for JSON request bodies. It targets a supplied path, adds JSON content headers, stringifies the body, and passes body metadata through for completion records.

symbols.does: Starts a streaming FormData image request.
symbols.description: This helper wraps streamSSE for multipart image-edit requests. It sends the supplied FormData with auth headers and passes an empty body metadata object to the stream parser.

symbols.does: Parses streamed image events and returns completed image records.
symbols.description: This async function performs a fetch, reads the response stream with TextDecoder, splits SSE-style blocks, parses data payloads as JSON, calls opts.onPartial for partial images, and collects completed base64 images. It throws normalized network and HTTP errors but ignores malformed event payloads.

symbols.does: Runs image generation through the Responses API image_generation tool.
symbols.description: This async function builds a /v1/responses request with user text and optional input images, optional previous_response_id, tool choice, and image-generation tool parameters. It extracts image_generation_call results into image records and returns the response id, images, revised prompt, and raw response.

symbols.does: Creates image variations through the /v1/images/variations endpoint.
symbols.description: This async function requires a configured key and source image, then uses the requested model only if its capabilities support variation. Otherwise it falls back to dall-e-2, submits FormData, and normalizes returned images.

symbols.does: Converts OpenAI image response items into a common image record shape.
symbols.description: This function returns url, base64 data URL, or unknown image records with prompt, size, type, and format fields. It defaults the output format to png when none is provided.

symbols.does: Checks the configured API connection by fetching /v1/models.
symbols.description: This async function validates key presence, fetches the model list, refreshes the image model cache using likely-image filtering plus registry entries, and returns counts for all models and image models. It converts network failures into a proxy-oriented cv-openai/network error.

symbols.does: Fetches a URL and returns its Blob body.
symbols.description: This compact async helper converts a remote or data-accessible URL into a Blob via fetch. It is exposed on window.cvOpenAI for callers that need image blobs for edit or variation flows.

symbols.does: Classifies chat text as image generation, image edit, or text intent.
symbols.description: This function lowercases the message and checks broad regex groups for creation verbs, edit verbs, and image-related nouns. It returns image-edit when edit language and image nouns are present, image-generate when creation language and image nouns are present, and text otherwise.
